lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer local whenever privacy, sovereignty, or operator-briefing needs control
selection_reasons: Primary supervisor lane for local-only or sensitive work.
Should stay available even when bulk local capacity is being harvested.
avoid_when: A cheaper sovereign bulk lane can satisfy the task without consuming the supervisor reserve.

lanes.roi_posture: use local paid-for compute before cloud when quality is sufficient
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer local for private code, migrations, and maintenance unless cloud review materially changes the outcome
selection_reasons: Retains enough reserve for interactive coding while still allowing harvested local work.
avoid_when: The task is explicitly cloud-safe and benefits materially from premium frontier implementation quality.

lanes.roi_posture: maximize productive use of idle local capacity after reserves are protected
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: strongly prefer local for extraction, indexing, creative batches, and batch transforms
selection_reasons: Primary lane for harvested local compute and sovereign creative batch work.
Should absorb batch work that would otherwise spend cloud budget with no quality advantage.
avoid_when: Interactive reserves are degraded or a protected creative reserve is needed immediately.

lanes.roi_posture: save for high-leverage implementation and final-review work
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: use when the coding delta is worth premium frontier quality or faster issue resolution
selection_reasons: Primary premium coding lane for cloud-safe implementation.
Protected so the reserve is still available for high-value coding spikes.
avoid_when: The work is local-only or a sovereign local coding lane can finish it without meaningful quality loss.

lanes.roi_posture: burn early for large-context audits before spending scarcer premium coding reserve
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer for large-context, cloud-safe audits that would overconsume premium implementation lanes
selection_reasons: Primary large-context audit and architecture-review lane.
Burn before premium implementation reserve when the task is analysis-heavy.
avoid_when: The task contains sovereign-only, refusal-sensitive, or adult-sensitive material.

lanes.roi_posture: route deterministic refactors through the cheapest policy-legal model family that still meets quality
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer local or cheap metered routing for mechanical refactors before premium frontier spend
selection_reasons: Permanent repo mechanic for structured multi-file patching.
Separates mechanical refactor economics from premium interactive reasoning lanes.
avoid_when: The task is an open-ended architecture problem rather than a deterministic code mechanic.

lanes.roi_posture: spend subscription-backed GLM coding quota before metered overflow when the supported tooling path is healthy
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer for cloud-safe bulk coding and refactor overflow once support tooling is proven live
selection_reasons: Target subscription-backed overflow lane for cloud-safe coding.
Should sit between premium cloud lanes and cheap metered APIs when tooling health is green.
avoid_when: Provider auth or supported-tool evidence is stale or failed.

lanes.roi_posture: use as the cheapest cloud-safe bulk lane inside acceptable-quality thresholds
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer for low-cost cloud-safe transforms, extraction, and background throughput when local reserves are protected
selection_reasons: Cheapest metered cloud lane for eligible bulk work.
Ideal overflow path for classification, extraction, and cheap background transforms.
avoid_when: The work requires premium implementation quality or is not cloud-safe.

lanes.roi_posture: use for bounded async research or background cloud-safe processing after higher-value lanes are protected
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: prefer local harvested capacity first unless async cloud breadth materially improves throughput
selection_reasons: Useful for deep research and async cloud-safe work, but not a protected premium reserve.
avoid_when: A local harvested lane can perform the work without privacy or quality loss.

lanes.roi_posture: retire after review coverage migrates cleanly to Gemini and Codex
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: current-state only until migration gates pass
selection_reasons: Retained only as a migration lane until review coverage fully transfers.
avoid_when: Target-state routing is available and healthy.

lanes.roi_posture: product runtime lane, not a general-purpose reasoning reserve
lanes.local_vs_cloud_opportunity_cost: only use for public-product async work that already belongs on Vercel
selection_reasons: Purpose-built product async lane for webhooks, cache refreshes, and transactional product work.
avoid_when: The task is internal, private, sovereign, or better served by Athanor or local compute.
